free_text: Excitation function of deuteron induced nuclear
reactions on nat-Ti for monitoring deuteron beam
Main reference.
Reference containing revised
data for 48V.
MGC 20E Cyclotron in Debrecen
CGR 560 Cyclotron in Brussels
CV28 Cyclotron in Juelic
12.1, 20.0, 21.6 and 50.0 Ti Foils from Goodfellow
5 mm collimated beam on target
The beam intensity was monitored continuously
during the irradiation and was kept constant within 5%.
Charge was collected in Faraday-cup and was monitored
with 27-Al(D,X)24-Na monitor reaction.
The flux determined by monitor reaction was 10% more
than that measured by Faraday cup.
In the calculation of cross section data the
charge integrated by Faraday-cup was used. Nuclear
data were taken from E.Browne and R.B.Firestone,Table
of Radioactive Isotopes,Ed.V.S.Shirley, Wiley 1986.
Targets were irradiated from 0.5 to 2 hours, with 200
to 300 nA deuteron beam with 9.7., 13.6, 16.8, 20.8
and 21.8 MeV primary energies.The mean energy in the
target foils was calculated on the basis of the tables
of H.H.Andersen,J.F.Ziegler,Hydrogen Stopping Powers
and Ranges in All Elements,Vol.3,Pergamon Press, New
York, 1977 and C.F.Williamson,J.P.Boujot,J.Picard,
Tables of Ranges and Stopping Power of Chemical Elem.
for Charged Particles of Energy 0.05 to 500. MeV,
Report CEA-R-3042.
The error of the energy was calculated taking
into account the initial uncertainty the straggling of
the beam and the errors in the thickness of the foils.
The error of the cross section data were
estimated by summing up the squares of the individual
relative errors and taking the square root of it.
-Number of Target Atoms               1%
-Nuclear Decay Data                   3%
-Effect of Beam Fluctuation           1%
-Calibration of Gamma-Spectometer     5%
-Statistical Error and Peak Fitting 1-10%
- Beam Intensity                      7%
Status Report on Cross Section of Monitor Reactions for
Radioisotope Production, IAEA-Report INDC(NDS)-218/GZ,
Vienna, Austria,1989.
Table 3 of the manuscript and approved by S. Takacs

were added which contain revised data for 48V.

ts. Uncertainty of DATA2 point at 2.95 MeV
was corrected, 0.00 -> 0.005, in subentry 003.

was corrected 0.00 -> 0.005

3.97 MeV corrected.
These results were obtained by the authors after a
strict revision of data of the main reference and were
reported in NIMB 174 (2001) 235.
Chapter 4
